title: Vent Baffles
desc: The catacomb air is running sharp with ozone again, which means the vent grilles and cloth baffles need attention.

steps.text:
The Conjurors pretend not to care what the rest of Mipedian society thinks of them, but even they care when the air stops circulating properly. The catacombs run on a system of old vent shafts, hanging cloth baffles, and grated side channels that carry heat, scent, and the metallic edge of Mugical work away from the busier chambers. Today the ozone has grown sharp enough to sting the back of your throat, which the vent keeper takes as an insult to both engineering and dignity.

She raps a knuckle on the nearest grille. "Scrape the salt bloom from the vent mouths if you want the grit job," she says. "Or retie the cloth baffles in the side run if you prefer ladders and fabric. Either way, I would like the catacombs to stop smelling as if lightning has decided to live here permanently."

options.label: Scrape the salt bloom from the vent grilles
Retie the hanging cloth baffles in the side run

steps.text:
The vent grilles are furred with pale mineral bloom that forms wherever damp air, trace salts, and too much reactive residue start negotiating with one another. You chip the buildup away, brush the slots clear, and test the airflow at each opening until the draft returns from reluctant whisper to steady pull. Once the blocked mouths are open again, the sharp electric smell starts thinning almost immediately.

The keeper stands still for a moment, head tilted, reading the tunnel with her skin the way some people read weather. The floral note returns first, then the cooler mineral scent of ordinary stone. "Better," she says. "The catacombs may remain unnerving. They need not also be stale."

options.label: Dump the scraped bloom into the waste crock and clear the run

steps.text:
The hanging baffles are strips of weighted cloth set in alternating lines to slow and direct the drafts without shutting them down. Two have twisted together and a third has torn loose from its upper ring, letting one side passage blast its full unpleasant breath into the corridor. You climb the side ladder, separate the tangled lengths, and retie the loose cloth so the baffle pattern falls back into its staggered rhythm.

As the cloth settles, the air does too. What had been one hard stream of charged breath turns back into layered movement, cooler low along the floor, lighter overhead, and far less aggressive to stand in. The keeper watches the fabric answer the corrected draft and gives a short approving sound. "Good. Even dangerous ideas deserve decent ventilation."

options.label: Climb down and leave the cloth line breathing properly
